title: Software Developer
text: A Software Developer designs, builds, and maintains the applications and systems that run modern products. Core work is programming, software design, debugging, and problem solving, plus the communication and collaboration needed to ship as a team. The role suits analytical, detail-oriented people who enjoy building things and reasoning through logic (Investigative and Conventional interests). Outlook is strong, growing roughly 25% through 2032, with an approximate US wage band of about $80k-$150k that varies widely by location and seniority. Common entry paths include computer science degrees or bootcamps; developers often move into data engineering, DevOps, engineering management, or product management, and arrive from QA, IT support, or analyst roles.

title: Data Scientist
text: A Data Scientist uses statistics, machine learning, and programming to turn large, messy datasets into predictions and insight. Daily work spans data cleaning, modeling, experiment design, and communicating results so decision-makers can act. The role fits curious, rigorous, analytical people who like open-ended problems (Investigative with a Conventional streak). Demand is among the fastest in tech, growing roughly 35% through 2032, with an approximate US wage band near $100k-$165k depending on industry and experience. Most hold quantitative degrees and strong coding skills. People often transition in from data analyst or research roles and move on to machine learning engineering, data engineering, or analytics leadership as they specialize.

title: Data Analyst
text: A Data Analyst gathers, cleans, and interprets data to answer everyday business questions and measure performance. Core skills are data analysis, SQL, data visualization, and clear communication, supported by critical thinking and comfort with spreadsheets and basic statistics. The role suits methodical, curious people who like finding the story in numbers and helping others decide (Investigative and Conventional interests). It is a common entry point into the data field, growing roughly 23% through 2032, with an approximate US wage band around $60k-$95k. Analysts frequently come from business, finance, or operations backgrounds and progress into data science, business intelligence, data engineering, or product roles.

title: UX Designer
text: A UX Designer shapes how people experience digital products, from research through wireframes, prototypes, and tested interfaces. Daily work blends user research, prototyping, visual design, usability testing, and constant communication with product and engineering. The role fits creative, empathetic, people-curious problem solvers (Artistic with an Investigative streak) who enjoy turning user needs into intuitive flows. Demand is solid, growing roughly 16% through 2032, with an approximate US wage band near $75k-$120k that varies by region and seniority. Designers often come from graphic design, research, or front-end backgrounds and move into product design, UX research, UI development, or product management as their scope grows.

title: Product Manager
text: A Product Manager decides what a product team should build and why, owning strategy, roadmap, and prioritization. The job centers on communication, strategic thinking, stakeholder management, and project management, with enough data analysis and user research to ground decisions in evidence. It suits persuasive, driven, big-picture people who like leading without direct authority (Enterprising with an Investigative streak). Outlook is moderate, growing roughly 7% through 2032, with an approximate US wage band around $100k-$160k that scales sharply with seniority. PMs often arrive from engineering, design, marketing, or analyst roles and advance to senior or director-level product, product marketing, or general management positions.

title: Data Engineer
text: A Data Engineer builds the pipelines, warehouses, and infrastructure that deliver clean, reliable data to analysts and machine-learning systems. Core skills are data pipeline design, programming, SQL, database management, and increasingly cloud platforms, plus problem solving and cross-team collaboration. The role fits systematic, detail-driven builders who like robust systems over ambiguous analysis (Investigative and Conventional interests). Demand is strong as companies invest in data platforms, growing roughly 9% through 2032, with an approximate US wage band near $95k-$155k. Engineers often transition in from software development or data analysis and move toward data architecture, platform engineering, or data science.

title: Cybersecurity Analyst
text: A Cybersecurity Analyst, also called an information security analyst, protects an organization's networks and data by monitoring for threats, investigating alerts, and responding to incidents. The work centers on network security, threat analysis, risk assessment, and incident response, with sharp attention to detail and the ability to explain risks clearly. It suits vigilant, methodical problem solvers who like defending systems (Investigative and Conventional interests). Demand is among the fastest in tech, growing roughly 32% through 2032, with an approximate US wage band near $80k-$130k. Analysts often come from IT support or network administration and advance into security engineering, penetration testing, or security architecture.

title: DevOps Engineer
text: A DevOps Engineer connects software development and IT operations by automating the pipelines and infrastructure that ship and run applications. Core skills are automation, cloud platforms, scripting, system administration, and monitoring, plus the collaboration to bridge teams. The role suits hands-on, practical problem solvers who like building and tuning reliable systems (Realistic with an Investigative streak). Demand is strong as cloud adoption grows, increasing roughly 9% through 2032, with an approximate US wage band near $95k-$150k. Engineers commonly arrive from software development or systems administration and progress into site reliability engineering, cloud architecture, or platform leadership.

title: Machine Learning Engineer
text: A Machine Learning Engineer builds, optimizes, and deploys ML systems so models run reliably in real products at scale. The role blends machine learning, strong programming, software design, and data analysis with growing use of cloud platforms, plus collaboration across data and engineering teams. It fits rigorous, hands-on builders who enjoy both research and engineering (Investigative with a Realistic streak). Demand is very strong amid the AI boom, growing roughly 26% through 2032, with an approximate US wage band near $110k-$180k that runs high at leading firms. Engineers often transition in from software development or data science and move toward AI research engineering, data engineering, or technical leadership.

title: Registered Nurse
text: A Registered Nurse (RN) delivers direct patient care in hospitals, clinics, and community settings, assessing conditions, administering medications, monitoring vital signs, and educating patients and families. Core skills include active listening, clear communication, critical thinking, attention to detail, and empathy, performed under time pressure and in close teamwork with physicians. The role fits Social and Investigative people who are caring, level-headed, and comfortable with both human contact and clinical detail. RNs typically need an associate or bachelor's degree (job zone 3) plus licensure. Outlook is steadily growing, around 6% through 2032, with approximate pay of $70k-$100k. Common transitions include advancing to Nurse Practitioner, Clinical Nurse Specialist, or Nurse Manager, or entering from Licensed Practical Nurse roles.

title: Nurse Practitioner
text: A Nurse Practitioner (NP) is an advanced-practice nurse who diagnoses conditions, orders and interprets tests, prescribes medications, and manages full treatment plans, frequently acting as a primary care provider. The role demands strong critical thinking, decision making, active listening, communication, and data analysis, blended with empathy for long-term patient relationships. It suits Investigative and Social people who enjoy clinical problem-solving alongside human care. NPs need a master's or doctoral degree and certification (job zone 5), with approximate pay of $110k-$140k. The outlook is exceptionally strong, growing roughly 40% through 2032. Most NPs advance from Registered Nurse roles; adjacent paths include Physician Assistant, Clinical Nurse Specialist, and Nurse Manager positions.

title: Physician Assistant
text: A Physician Assistant (PA) practices medicine collaboratively with physicians, examining patients, diagnosing illness, ordering and interpreting tests, prescribing medication, and building treatment plans across many specialties. The role requires critical thinking, decision making, communication, active listening, and data analysis with high attention to detail. It fits Investigative and Social people who want broad clinical scope and direct patient contact. PAs complete a master's-level program and certification (job zone 5), earning approximately $105k-$135k. The outlook is very strong, around 27% growth through 2032. PAs often move between specialties or toward Nurse Practitioner work; adjacent roles include Registered Nurse, Surgical Technologist, and Medical Assistant.

title: Physical Therapist
text: A Physical Therapist (PT) helps people recover movement and manage pain after injury, surgery, or illness by evaluating function and prescribing exercise and manual-therapy programs. Key skills include communication, active listening, critical thinking, problem solving, empathy, and patience across repeated sessions. The role suits Social and Investigative people who like hands-on, motivating, long-term patient work. PTs need a Doctor of Physical Therapy degree and licensure (job zone 5), earning approximately $85k-$110k. The outlook is strong, roughly 15% growth through 2032. Adjacent roles include Occupational Therapist and Athletic Trainer, with many entering after working as a Physical Therapist Assistant or in nursing.

title: Medical and Health Services Manager
text: A Medical and Health Services Manager runs the operational side of healthcare organizations, overseeing budgets, staffing, regulatory compliance, and quality and efficiency metrics for clinics, hospitals, or departments. The role leans on project management, leadership, decision making, communication, and data analysis. It fits Enterprising and Social people who enjoy organizing teams and systems rather than hands-on clinical work. A bachelor's or master's degree (job zone 4) is typical, with approximate pay of $100k-$135k. The outlook is excellent, around 28% growth through 2032. Many enter from clinical roles like Registered Nurse or from Health Information Manager and Operations Manager positions, often advancing to Clinical Director.

title: Diagnostic Medical Sonographer
text: A Diagnostic Medical Sonographer uses ultrasound equipment to produce images of organs, tissues, and blood flow that physicians rely on for diagnosis. The role demands strong attention to detail, technical proficiency, critical thinking, and clear communication with patients and clinicians. It suits Realistic and Investigative people who like operating precise equipment while caring for patients. An associate degree or certificate (job zone 3) is typical, with approximate pay of $70k-$95k. The outlook is strong, about 14% growth through 2032. Adjacent imaging roles include Radiologic Technologist, Cardiovascular Technologist, and MRI Technologist, and some transition in from or out to Registered Nurse work.

title: Pharmacist
text: A Pharmacist dispenses medications, verifies dosing and interactions, and counsels patients on safe and effective use, while advising prescribers on drug choices. The role centers on attention to detail, communication, critical thinking, active listening, and decision making. It fits Investigative and Social people who value accuracy and trusted advice. Pharmacists earn a Doctor of Pharmacy degree and licensure (job zone 5), with approximate pay of $120k-$140k. The outlook is roughly flat to slightly positive, about 3% growth through 2032. Common adjacent paths include advancing from Pharmacy Technician, specializing as a Clinical Pharmacist, or moving into Medical Science Liaison roles in industry.

title: Respiratory Therapist
text: A Respiratory Therapist evaluates and treats patients with breathing and cardiopulmonary disorders, managing ventilators, delivering oxygen and aerosol medications, and running lung-function tests, often in emergencies. Key skills are critical thinking, attention to detail, communication, technical proficiency, and empathy. The role suits Social and Realistic people who stay calm under pressure and like hands-on equipment work with patients. An associate or bachelor's degree plus licensure is typical (job zone 3), with approximate pay of $65k-$90k. The outlook is strong, about 13% growth through 2032. Adjacent roles include Registered Nurse, Cardiovascular Technologist, and Sleep or Polysomnographic technologist positions.

title: Mental Health Counselor
text: A Mental Health Counselor provides talk therapy to individuals, couples, and groups facing anxiety, depression, trauma, addiction, and life transitions. The work centers on active listening, empathy, clear communication, structured counseling techniques, and careful documentation, often using evidence-based methods like cognitive behavioral therapy. It suits Social and Investigative people who are patient, emotionally steady, and genuinely curious about how others think and heal. Most roles require a master's degree plus supervised clinical hours and state licensure. The outlook is strong, growing roughly 18% through 2032 as demand for behavioral health expands, with typical pay in the $45k-$75k range. Counselors commonly move in from social work or teaching and move on to private practice, clinical supervision, marriage and family therapy, or substance abuse counseling.

title: Clinical Psychologist
text: A Clinical Psychologist assesses, diagnoses, and treats mental, emotional, and behavioral disorders using standardized testing, clinical interviews, and evidence-based psychotherapy. The role draws heavily on psychological assessment, diagnosis, data analysis, research methods, and strong communication, and it suits Investigative and Social people who enjoy rigorous reasoning paired with deep human contact. It typically requires a doctoral degree (PhD or PsyD), a supervised internship, and state licensure. Employment is projected to grow about 6% through 2032, with typical pay around $80k-$130k depending on setting and specialty. Many enter after master's-level counseling work and graduate training, and they often branch into neuropsychology, forensic or health psychology, academia and research, or independent private practice over a career.

title: Social Worker
text: A Social Worker helps individuals, families, and communities cope with challenges by assessing needs, coordinating resources, and advocating across healthcare, housing, child welfare, and benefit systems. Core skills include case management, active listening, communication, advocacy, resource coordination, and thorough documentation. The work fits Social and Enterprising people who are organized, resilient, and driven to help vulnerable populations navigate bureaucracy. Direct-service roles often require a bachelor's in social work, while clinical practice needs a master's (MSW) and licensure. Employment is projected to grow about 7% through 2032, with typical pay near $45k-$70k. People frequently enter from counseling, nonprofit, or community-health backgrounds and move on to clinical social work, healthcare or school settings, program supervision, or community service management.

title: Substance Abuse and Behavioral Disorder Counselor
text: A Substance Abuse and Behavioral Disorder Counselor helps clients overcome addictions and compulsive behaviors through individual and group counseling, recovery planning, and relapse prevention. The role relies on active listening, empathy, counseling techniques, group facilitation, crisis intervention, and careful documentation. It suits Social and Investigative people who are nonjudgmental, steady under stress, and motivated by recovery and behavior change. Entry can begin with a bachelor's or certification, though clinical roles often require a master's and licensure. Demand is strong, growing roughly 18% through 2032 as treatment access expands, with typical pay around $40k-$65k. Counselors commonly enter from peer-support, social work, or general counseling roles and advance into clinical supervision, program management, or broader mental health counseling.

title: Marriage and Family Therapist
text: A Marriage and Family Therapist treats emotional and relational problems by working with couples and families as a system rather than only with individuals. Key skills include active listening, conflict resolution, counseling techniques, empathy, communication, and treatment planning. The role fits Social and Investigative people who are perceptive about group dynamics, comfortable mediating tension, and patient through slow relational change. It generally requires a master's degree, supervised clinical hours, and state licensure. The outlook is strong, growing about 15% through 2032, with typical pay around $50k-$80k. Practitioners often transition in from mental health counseling or social work and move on to private practice, clinical supervision, or specialized work in trauma, addiction, or child and adolescent therapy.

title: School and Career Counselor
text: A School and Career Counselor supports students' academic progress, social-emotional growth, and career planning across K-12 and college settings. The work centers on active listening, advising, communication, assessment, and advocacy, often using interest inventories to guide students toward fitting paths. It suits Social and Artistic people who are encouraging, organized, and energized by adolescent and young-adult development. Most positions require a master's degree and, in schools, a state credential. Employment is projected to grow about 5% through 2032, with typical pay near $50k-$75k. People often enter from teaching or counseling backgrounds and transition into clinical mental health counseling, career coaching, student-services leadership, or education administration as they gain experience.

title: Healthcare Social Worker
text: A Healthcare Social Worker helps patients and families manage the emotional, practical, and financial sides of illness, injury, and end-of-life care within medical settings. The role draws on case management, resource coordination, active listening, advocacy, communication, and documentation, often under time pressure during discharge planning. It fits Social and Investigative people who stay calm in clinical environments and are skilled at navigating insurance, benefits, and family dynamics. Most clinical roles require an MSW and licensure. Employment is projected to grow about 10% through 2032 as the population ages, with typical pay around $50k-$75k. Many enter from general social work and move into hospice leadership, care coordination, clinical counseling, or healthcare program management.

title: Community and Social Service Manager
text: A Community and Social Service Manager leads the programs and teams that deliver social services, from nonprofits and clinics to government agencies. The role centers on program management, team leadership, budgeting, communication, resource coordination, and outcome data analysis. It suits Enterprising and Social people who can balance mission with administration, manage staff and funders, and turn community needs into sustainable programs. It typically requires a bachelor's or master's plus several years of direct-service experience. Employment is projected to grow about 9% through 2032, with typical pay around $60k-$95k. Managers usually rise from front-line social work or counseling roles and can advance into executive nonprofit leadership, policy and program analysis, or broader operations management.

title: Financial Analyst
text: A Financial Analyst studies financial data, company filings, and market trends to guide investment, budgeting, and business decisions. Core skills are financial analysis, data analysis, statistics, and clear communication of findings, with strong attention to detail. The role suits Conventional and Enterprising people who enjoy working with numbers, building models, and forming evidence-based opinions; it rewards rigor, curiosity, and comfort with spreadsheets. The outlook is steady growth of roughly 8% through 2032, with an approximate US wage band of $70k-$130k depending on industry and seniority. People often enter from accounting, data analysis, or economics backgrounds and move on to roles such as Budget Analyst, portfolio or fund management, Operations Manager, or finance leadership over time.

title: Accountant
text: An Accountant prepares, examines, and reconciles financial records to keep an organization's books accurate and compliant with tax and reporting rules. The work centers on accounting knowledge, very high attention to detail, financial analysis, and steady communication with colleagues and auditors. It fits Conventional, detail-loving people who value order, accuracy, and structured processes, and who find satisfaction in getting the numbers to reconcile. The outlook is roughly 4% growth through 2032, a stable field, with an approximate US wage band of $55k-$100k that rises with certification such as a CPA. Common adjacent roles include Financial Analyst, Auditor, Bookkeeper, and over time Controller or finance manager positions.

title: Operations Manager
text: An Operations Manager runs the day-to-day functioning of a team, facility, or business unit, balancing people, budgets, and processes to meet goals. The role leans on operations know-how, leadership, people management, project management, and logistics, plus clear communication across departments. It suits Enterprising and Conventional people who like organizing work, leading teams, and steadily improving how things run under real constraints. The outlook is roughly 6% growth through 2032, with an approximate US wage band of $80k-$130k depending on company size and sector. People often arrive from project management, supervisory, or analyst roles and progress to General Manager, Supply Chain Manager, or director and executive operations positions.

title: Project Manager
text: A Project Manager plans and delivers projects on scope, schedule, and budget, coordinating people and resources from kickoff to close. The role depends on project management discipline, strong communication, leadership, people management, and problem solving under shifting constraints. It fits Enterprising and Conventional people who like organizing complex work, aligning stakeholders, and driving things to completion. The outlook is roughly 7% growth through 2032, with an approximate US wage band of $75k-$120k that varies by industry and certification such as PMP. People commonly enter from coordinator, analyst, or operations backgrounds and move into Program Manager, Operations Manager, Product Manager, or PMO leadership roles.

title: Management Consultant
text: A Management Consultant, also called a management analyst, helps organizations solve problems and improve efficiency, structure, and strategy. The work hinges on strategy, problem solving, data analysis, and persuasive communication, supported by project management and financial reasoning. It suits Enterprising and Investigative people who enjoy analyzing complex situations, advising leaders, and shaping recommendations across many industries. The outlook is strong, around 10% growth through 2032, with an approximate US wage band of $80k-$150k that scales sharply with firm and seniority. People enter from analyst, finance, or operations roles, sometimes after an MBA, and move into internal strategy, Operations Manager, Product Manager, or executive positions.

title: Human Resources Manager
text: A Human Resources Manager oversees recruiting, employee relations, compensation, and compliance, shaping how a company hires, develops, and retains people. The role draws on people management, communication, leadership, active listening, and negotiation, with growing use of HR data and strategy. It fits Enterprising and Social people who like working with others, mediating conflict, and building fair, effective workplace systems. The outlook is roughly 6% growth through 2032, with an approximate US wage band of $80k-$140k depending on organization size. People often enter from recruiting, HR generalist, or operations roles and progress to Training Manager, HR Business Partner, or HR Director and VP positions.

title: Marketing Manager
text: A Marketing Manager plans and leads campaigns and brand strategy to grow demand and reach for a company's products or services. The role combines marketing expertise, strategy, persuasive communication, leadership, and increasingly data analysis to measure what works. It suits Enterprising and Artistic people who enjoy creative problem solving, leading teams, and connecting messaging to business results. The outlook is roughly 7% growth through 2032, with an approximate US wage band of $90k-$160k that varies widely by industry and company stage. People typically enter from marketing specialist, content, or analyst roles and advance to Brand Manager, Product Manager, Sales Manager, or marketing director and CMO positions.

title: Supply Chain Analyst
text: A Supply Chain Analyst, often titled logistician, studies how materials, inventory, and products flow through a business to make sourcing and delivery faster and cheaper. The role relies on logistics knowledge, data analysis, operations understanding, attention to detail, and problem solving. It fits Conventional and Enterprising people who like working with data, optimizing systems, and solving practical bottlenecks. The outlook is roughly 6% growth through 2032, with an approximate US wage band of $60k-$95k depending on sector and scope. People commonly enter from logistics coordination, data analysis, or operations roles and move into Operations Manager, Procurement Manager, or supply chain leadership positions.

title: Sales Representative
text: A Sales Representative sells products or services directly to businesses or consumers, finding prospects, presenting value, handling objections, and closing deals. Core skills are selling, negotiation, clear communication, customer service, and active listening to understand what a buyer actually needs. It suits Enterprising and Social people who enjoy persuading, building relationships, and the energy of hitting targets, and who can handle rejection and a commission-driven income. The outlook is holding steady at about 4% through 2032, with an approximate US base wage band of $45k-$90k before commission, which can lift total pay considerably. Reps commonly move up into account management or sales leadership, or sideways into marketing and customer success.

title: Sales Manager
text: A Sales Manager leads a team of sales reps, setting quotas, designing territories, forecasting revenue, and coaching people to perform. The role blends selling instinct with leadership, people management, negotiation, communication, and the strategy to allocate effort where it pays off. It fits Enterprising, organized (Conventional) people who like driving results through others and thrive on accountability for a number. The outlook is growing steadily at roughly 5% through 2032, with an approximate US wage band around $95k-$160k including incentives. Most arrive after success as a Sales Representative or account manager, and from here people move into broader commercial leadership, operations management, or general marketing roles.

title: Market Research Analyst
text: A Market Research Analyst studies markets, customers, and competitors to tell a company what to sell, to whom, and at what price. The work centers on data analysis, research methods, statistics, clear communication, and enough marketing sense to make findings actionable. It suits Investigative, detail-oriented (Conventional) people who like digging into numbers and patterns and translating them into recommendations. The outlook is strong, growing fast at roughly 13% through 2032, with an approximate US wage band around $60k-$95k. Analysts often come from data analyst or research backgrounds and move on into marketing management, product marketing, or strategy roles where the insight they produce gets put into action.

title: Digital Marketing Specialist
text: A Digital Marketing Specialist runs a brand's online presence, building and optimizing campaigns across search, social, email, and paid advertising. The role mixes marketing craft with data analysis, writing, communication, and the strategy to decide where budget and creative effort go. It fits Artistic and Enterprising people who enjoy both making campaigns and chasing measurable results, comfortable iterating fast on what the metrics say. The outlook is growing strongly at about 10% through 2032, with an approximate US wage band around $50k-$85k. People enter from content, copywriting, or analytics roles, and progress toward marketing management, product marketing, or specialized paths like SEO and growth.

title: Public Relations Specialist
text: A Public Relations Specialist manages how an organization is perceived, crafting press materials, pitching stories to media, and protecting reputation during sensitive moments. Core skills are persuasive communication, sharp writing, storytelling, strategy, and the marketing sense to align messages with brand goals. It suits Enterprising and Artistic people who like influencing perception, building media relationships, and staying calm under pressure. The outlook is growing steadily at roughly 6% through 2032, with an approximate US wage band around $50k-$85k. Specialists often come from journalism, writing, or content roles and move on into communications leadership, broader marketing management, or social and brand functions.

title: Social Media Manager
text: A Social Media Manager owns a brand's voice and presence across social platforms, planning content, creating posts, engaging followers, and growing an audience. The role blends marketing and storytelling with communication, writing, and enough data analysis to read engagement and refine the approach. It fits Artistic and Enterprising people who enjoy creating, connecting with a community, and reacting quickly to trends. The outlook is growing strongly at roughly 10% through 2032, with an approximate US wage band around $50k-$80k. Many arrive from content or digital marketing roles, and move on toward marketing management, broader digital strategy, or communications and PR functions.

title: Account Manager
text: An Account Manager nurtures a company's existing customer relationships, ensuring clients stay satisfied, renew, and grow their spend. The role leans on relationship selling, strong communication, customer service, negotiation, and active listening to anticipate client needs. It suits Enterprising and Social people who prefer building long-term trust over chasing brand-new logos, and who enjoy being the dependable point of contact. The outlook is holding steady at about 4% through 2032, with an approximate US wage band around $55k-$100k including incentives. People typically come from sales rep or customer-facing roles and move on into customer success, sales management, or marketing as they broaden their commercial view.

title: Teacher (K–12)
text: A Teacher (K–12) plans and delivers standards-aligned lessons to elementary or secondary students, assesses their progress, manages a classroom, and partners with families. Core skills are teaching, clear communication, curriculum design, active listening, empathy, and classroom management. It suits Social and Artistic people who are patient, organized, and energized by helping young people grow. The outlook is roughly flat (about 1% through 2032), though demand varies sharply by subject and region, with shortages in math, science, and special education. Typical pay runs about $50k-$80k. Common entry points are paraprofessional, tutoring, or a teaching-credential program; teachers often move into instructional design, school counseling, curriculum development, or administration.

title: Instructional Designer
text: An Instructional Designer turns subject-matter content into structured courses, e-learning modules, and assessments for schools or corporate training. The work blends curriculum design, clear writing, project management, communication, learner research, and light data analysis to measure whether instruction works. It fits Artistic and Investigative people who like systematic problem-solving, building things, and shaping how others learn. Demand is growing steadily (about 7% through 2032), driven by online and corporate learning. Pay is roughly $60k-$95k, higher in tech and corporate settings. People often arrive from classroom teaching, training, or content roles, and move on to training-and-development, curriculum development, learning-experience leadership, or UX design.

title: Postsecondary Professor
text: A Postsecondary Professor teaches college and university courses, designs curricula, advises and mentors students, and (at research institutions) conducts and publishes scholarship. The role draws on deep subject expertise, teaching, research methods, academic writing, public speaking, and mentoring. It suits Investigative and Social people who love their field, enjoy explaining ideas, and tolerate long training and a competitive job market. Overall demand grows steadily (about 8% through 2032), but tenure-track positions are scarce and many roles are adjunct. Pay varies widely, roughly $70k-$130k by field and rank. Entrants usually hold a master's or doctorate; professors transition into research, administration, instructional design, or industry expert roles.

title: Special Education Teacher
text: A Special Education Teacher tailors instruction for students with disabilities, develops and monitors individualized education programs (IEPs), and coordinates with families, therapists, and general-education staff. The work hinges on teaching, deep empathy, active listening, individualized assessment, and patient problem-solving. It fits Social and Investigative people who are resilient, organized, and motivated by helping students who learn differently. Demand is about flat overall (around 1% through 2032) but persistent shortages make jobs widely available. Pay is roughly $50k-$80k. People often enter from general teaching or paraprofessional roles and move into counseling, instructional design, advocacy, or school administration.

title: Training and Development Specialist
text: A Training and Development Specialist identifies workforce skill gaps and designs, delivers, and evaluates employee training, onboarding, and workshops in corporate or institutional settings. The role blends facilitation and teaching, strong communication, curriculum design, project management, public speaking, and basic data analysis to measure impact. It suits Social and Enterprising people who enjoy coaching adults, organizing programs, and presenting. Demand grows steadily (about 6% through 2032) as employers invest in upskilling. Pay is roughly $55k-$90k. Many enter from teaching, HR, or subject-expert roles and advance into instructional design, training management, or learning-and-development leadership.

title: Education Administrator (Principal)
text: An Education Administrator, such as a school principal, leads a school's staff, budget, instruction, and culture, supporting teachers while managing operations, compliance, and community relations. The role centers on leadership, communication, people management, project management, and strategy under steady pressure. It suits Enterprising and Social people who are decisive, organized, and energized by developing teams and students. Demand is roughly flat (about 1% through 2032) but turnover keeps openings available. Pay is typically $85k-$120k. Most principals come from classroom teaching plus an administrative credential, and move into district leadership, superintendency, or operations roles outside K–12.

title: School Counselor
text: A School Counselor supports students' academic, social, emotional, and college or career development, providing guidance, short-term counseling, crisis screening, and referrals within a school. Core skills are active listening, empathy, communication, counseling technique, and assessment. It fits Social and Investigative people who are warm, perceptive, and steady under emotional load. Demand grows steadily (about 5% through 2032) as schools expand student-support services. Pay is roughly $50k-$80k. The path usually requires a master's in school counseling; counselors transition into mental-health or clinical counseling, social work, teaching, or school administration.

title: Corporate Trainer
text: A Corporate Trainer delivers hands-on employee training, such as product, sales, compliance, or soft-skills programs, through live workshops, webinars, and on-the-job coaching. The job leans on public speaking, communication, facilitation and teaching, and reading the room to keep adult learners engaged. It suits Social and Enterprising people who are confident presenters, adaptable, and people-energized. Demand grows steadily (about 6% through 2032) as companies upskill staff. Pay is roughly $50k-$85k, with higher ranges in technical and sales-heavy fields. Trainers often come from sales, support, or subject-expert roles and advance into instructional design, training development, or learning-program management.

title: Graphic Designer
text: Graphic Designer is a Creative, Media & Design role that turns ideas into visual communication: logos, layouts, packaging, ads, and digital graphics. Core skills are visual design, typography and color sense, broad design and layout judgment, attention to detail, and communication to interpret briefs and present work. It suits artistic, hands-on people (Holland A/R) who enjoy making polished, expressive artifacts and can take feedback. The outlook is roughly flat at about 3% through 2032 as demand shifts toward digital and motion work, with pay commonly around $45k-$80k. People often enter from art, illustration, or marketing backgrounds and move into UX design, art direction, web or brand design as they grow.

title: Content Strategist
text: Content Strategist is a Creative, Media & Design role focused on planning and governing content so it meets audience needs and business goals. The work blends strategy, strong writing, communication, project management, light data analysis, and marketing sense to set voice, structure, editorial calendars, and standards across web and product. It fits artistic yet enterprising people (Holland A/E) who like shaping systems and persuading teams, not just producing copy. The outlook is modest growth of about 4% through 2032, with pay commonly around $60k-$100k. Many arrive from writing, editing, or marketing roles and move on to marketing management, UX writing, or product management.

title: Writer / Editor
text: Writer / Editor is a Creative, Media & Design role that produces and polishes written content for media, marketing, and products. Core skills are writing and editing, storytelling, communication, research methods, and sharp attention to detail and house style. It suits artistic, investigative people (Holland A/I) who love language, accuracy, and turning research into clear narrative, and who can take and give feedback gracefully. The outlook is modest growth of about 4% through 2032, with pay commonly around $50k-$95k and wide variance by medium. People often come from journalism, communications, or subject-matter backgrounds and move into content strategy, technical or UX writing, or communications leadership.

title: Art Director
text: Art Director is a senior Creative, Media & Design role that owns the visual vision of a project and leads the people who execute it. The work needs strong visual design judgment plus leadership, strategy, communication, and project management to direct teams and sell concepts to clients. It fits artistic, enterprising people (Holland A/E) who can hold a creative standard while guiding others rather than doing all the hands-on craft. The outlook is modest growth of about 4% through 2032, with pay commonly around $80k-$130k. Art directors usually rise from graphic design, illustration, or copy roles and advance into creative direction or broader marketing leadership.

title: UX / Product Designer
growth: growing faster than average, about 13% through 2032
text: UX / Product Designer is a Creative, Media & Design role that designs how digital products feel and function, blending research, interaction design, and visual craft. Core skills are UX research, visual and interaction design, product sense, communication, and problem solving across flows, wireframes, and prototypes. It suits artistic, investigative people (Holland A/I) who enjoy both empathy for users and structured problem solving alongside engineers and PMs. The outlook is stronger than most creative roles at about 13% through 2032, with pay commonly around $75k-$120k. People enter from graphic design, research, or front-end backgrounds and move into product management, design leadership, or UX research.

title: Photographer
text: Photographer is a Creative, Media & Design role that captures and edits images for events, products, portraits, editorial, or commercial clients. The work combines a strong visual eye, hands-on camera and lighting skill, attention to detail, storytelling, and post-production editing, plus customer service for the many who freelance. It fits artistic, hands-on people (Holland A/R) comfortable with gear, irregular schedules, and running a small business. The outlook is modest growth of about 4% through 2032, with pay commonly around $30k-$70k and wide variance by specialty and self-employment. Many expand into video, photo editing, design, or art direction.

title: Video Editor / Producer
growth: growing faster than average, about 7% through 2032
text: Video Editor / Producer is a Creative, Media & Design role that shapes footage, sound, and graphics into finished video for film, advertising, social, and streaming. Core skills are editing, storytelling and pacing, visual design, attention to detail, and project management to hit deadlines and budgets. It suits artistic, hands-on people (Holland A/R) who like sustained focus at the timeline and collaborating with directors and clients. Demand is rising with video everywhere, with growth around 7% through 2032 and pay commonly around $45k-$90k. People come from film, journalism, or motion backgrounds and move into motion design, directing, or content strategy.

title: Motion Graphics Designer
growth: growing faster than average, about 8% through 2032
text: Motion Graphics Designer is a Creative, Media & Design role that brings graphics to life through animation for ads, explainers, titles, UI, and games. The work fuses strong visual and graphic design with storytelling, editing, timing, and problem solving to make motion feel purposeful. It fits artistic, hands-on people (Holland A/R) who enjoy detailed craft at the intersection of design and video. Demand is healthy as brands lean on animated content, with growth around 8% through 2032 and pay commonly around $55k-$100k. People typically come from graphic design or video editing and can move into art direction, video production, or product/UI design.

title: Public Relations / Communications Specialist
text: Public Relations / Communications Specialist is a Creative, Media & Design role that manages how an organization tells its story and handles media and the public. Core skills are persuasive communication, writing, storytelling, strategy, project management, and public speaking to craft messages, pitch press, and steer reputation. It fits enterprising, artistic people (Holland E/A) who are comfortable with people, deadlines, and high-stakes messaging. The outlook is modest growth of about 6% through 2032, with pay commonly around $50k-$90k. People often come from journalism, writing, or marketing backgrounds and advance into content strategy, marketing management, or communications leadership.

title: Mechanical Engineer
text: A Mechanical Engineer designs, analyzes, and tests mechanical and thermal systems including engines, machinery, HVAC, and consumer products. Core skills are engineering design, computer-aided design (CAD), data analysis, simulation, and problem solving, paired with clear communication to coordinate with manufacturing and project teams. The role suits practical, investigative people (Realistic and Investigative) who like building tangible things, reasoning about physics, and iterating prototypes. Outlook is solid, growing roughly 10% through 2032, with broad demand across automotive, energy, aerospace, and industrial sectors. Typical US pay runs about $75k-$120k, higher with seniority or management. People usually enter from mechanical engineering degrees and move into manufacturing engineering, aerospace, mechatronics, product design, or engineering management; some pivot into project management or technical sales.

title: Electrical Engineer
text: An Electrical Engineer designs and develops electrical systems and components, from power distribution and motor controls to circuit boards and embedded electronics. Key skills include engineering design, circuit analysis, simulation, data analysis, and problem solving, plus communication to coordinate across hardware and manufacturing teams. The work fits Realistic and Investigative people who enjoy precise technical reasoning, troubleshooting, and turning theory into working hardware. Demand is steady, growing about 9% through 2032, driven by power, electronics, telecom, and renewable energy. Typical US pay is roughly $80k-$130k, rising with experience or leadership. Entrants usually hold electrical engineering degrees; common moves are into electronics, controls, power systems, or RF engineering, and onward into engineering management, systems engineering, or technical product roles.

title: Civil Engineer
text: A Civil Engineer plans, designs, and supervises infrastructure projects including roads, bridges, water and drainage systems, and buildings. The role draws on engineering design, project management, data analysis, problem solving, and strong communication, plus attention to codes and regulatory compliance. It suits Realistic, Investigative, and Conventional people who like structured technical work with real-world public impact and a mix of office and field time. The outlook is stable, growing roughly 5% through 2032 as aging infrastructure is repaired and rebuilt. Typical US pay is about $70k-$110k. Many enter via civil engineering degrees and the EIT/PE licensure path, then move into structural, transportation, geotechnical, or environmental specialties, or into construction management and project engineering leadership.

title: Aerospace Engineer
text: An Aerospace Engineer designs, models, and tests aircraft, spacecraft, satellites, and propulsion systems. The role relies on engineering design, advanced data analysis, simulation, CAD, and rigorous problem solving, with communication to coordinate across aerodynamics, structures, and controls teams. It fits Realistic and Investigative people drawn to physics-heavy, high-precision work where safety margins matter. Outlook is positive, growing about 6% through 2032 amid commercial aviation, defense, and the expanding space sector. Typical US pay runs roughly $90k-$150k, among the higher engineering bands. Entrants usually hold aerospace or mechanical engineering degrees; people transition in from mechanical or systems engineering and move into propulsion, flight test, systems engineering, or program and engineering management.

title: Chemical Engineer
text: A Chemical Engineer designs and optimizes the processes that turn raw materials into products like fuels, chemicals, pharmaceuticals, and food. The work centers on engineering design, process optimization, data analysis, and problem solving, with communication to align operations and safety teams. It suits Investigative and Realistic people who enjoy chemistry, thermodynamics, and systematically improving complex processes. Outlook is favorable, growing roughly 8% through 2032 across energy, materials, pharma, and specialty chemicals. Typical US pay is about $85k-$135k. Most enter with chemical engineering degrees and move between process, manufacturing, environmental, and quality engineering roles, advancing into plant operations leadership, process safety, or engineering management; some pivot toward data-driven process analytics.

title: Architect
text: An Architect designs buildings and spaces, balancing aesthetics, function, safety, sustainability, and budget. The role blends creative design with project management, communication, CAD and 3D modeling, problem solving, and knowledge of building codes and regulatory compliance. It fits Artistic, Investigative, and Realistic people who want to combine visual creativity with technical rigor and client collaboration. Outlook is modest but steady, growing about 5% through 2032, tied to construction and real-estate cycles. Typical US pay runs roughly $70k-$120k, higher for principals and licensed leads. Entry requires an accredited architecture degree, internship hours, and licensure exams; common moves are into urban planning, interior design, construction management, or specialized practice such as sustainable or landscape design.

title: Industrial Engineer
text: An Industrial Engineer improves how organizations produce goods and deliver services by designing efficient systems of people, machines, materials, and information. Core skills are process optimization, data analysis, project management, problem solving, and communication, applied through lean and continuous-improvement methods. The role suits Realistic, Investigative, and Enterprising people who like analyzing workflows, cutting waste, and driving measurable efficiency gains. Outlook is strong, growing roughly 12% through 2032 as firms pursue productivity and supply-chain resilience. Typical US pay is about $70k-$115k. Entrants usually hold industrial or systems engineering degrees and move readily between manufacturing engineering, quality, supply chain, and operations roles, often advancing into operations management, continuous-improvement leadership, or data and analytics positions.

title: Environmental Engineer
text: An Environmental Engineer uses engineering principles to protect the environment and public health, working on water and wastewater treatment, air quality, waste management, and site remediation. The job draws on engineering design, data analysis, regulatory compliance, problem solving, and communication with regulators and stakeholders. It suits Investigative and Realistic people motivated by sustainability and applied science with field and modeling work. Outlook is steady, growing about 7% through 2032, supported by climate, water, and pollution-control priorities. Typical US pay is roughly $70k-$115k. Most enter via environmental or civil engineering degrees; common transitions include water resources, civil, or chemical engineering, and moves into sustainability consulting, environmental compliance, or policy and program management.

title: Research Scientist
text: A Research Scientist designs studies, runs experiments, and analyzes data to advance knowledge in a chosen field, then writes up findings for publication and grants. The core skills are research methods, data analysis, statistics, and clear technical writing, paired with patience and rigor. It fits curious, investigative people who enjoy deep, methodical problem-solving and don't mind long timelines or uncertain outcomes. Most roles expect an advanced degree (job zone 5). The outlook is steady, roughly 10% growth through 2032, with pay generally in the $80k-$130k range depending on sector and seniority. People often move in from postdoctoral research or lab technician roles, and out toward data science, biostatistics, or applied R&D leadership.

title: Biologist
text: A Biologist studies organisms and living systems through lab experiments or fieldwork, gathering samples, running analyses, and documenting results. Day to day blends hands-on lab techniques with research methods, careful data analysis, and meticulous attention to detail. It suits investigative, realistic people who like the natural world and methodical, evidence-based work. Most positions favor an advanced degree (job zone 5), though some lab roles start with a bachelor's. Demand grows modestly, around 7% through 2032, with wages roughly $65k-$100k depending on specialty and sector. Common moves are in from lab technician work and out toward research scientist, environmental science, or biostatistics roles.

title: Chemist
text: A Chemist studies the composition and behavior of substances, running lab experiments and analytical instruments to develop or test materials, formulations, and processes. The work centers on strong lab techniques, exacting attention to detail, research methods, and data analysis. It fits investigative, realistic people who enjoy precise, hands-on problem-solving and reproducible results. Many roles open with a bachelor's degree (job zone 4), with research-heavy posts wanting more. Growth is slow but stable, about 6% through 2032, and pay typically runs $60k-$100k by sector. People often enter from lab technician roles and move out into research science, materials science, or quality and process analysis.

title: Environmental Scientist
text: An Environmental Scientist samples and studies air, water, soil, and ecosystems to assess environmental quality, then advises on compliance and cleanup. The job mixes research methods and data analysis with field and lab techniques, plus clear report writing and communication with agencies and clients. It suits investigative, realistic people who care about the planet and like fieldwork balanced with analysis. A bachelor's degree is the typical entry (job zone 4). The outlook is strong, roughly 16% growth through 2032, with wages around $60k-$95k. Common transitions are in from biology or geoscience and out toward research science, sustainability analysis, or environmental consulting and policy.

title: Physicist
text: A Physicist investigates the fundamental laws of matter and energy through theory, experiment, and computational simulation, often building models and analyzing large datasets. The core skills are research methods, statistics, data analysis, deep problem-solving, and increasingly programming. It fits highly investigative, realistic minds who love abstract reasoning and rigorous quantitative work. The field expects an advanced degree (job zone 5). Demand is strong, around 20% growth through 2032, with pay among the higher science bands at roughly $100k-$160k. People typically arrive via postdoctoral research and move out into data science, quantitative finance, software engineering, or applied R&D when they leave academia.

title: Geoscientist
text: A Geoscientist studies the Earth's structure and processes to locate resources, assess hazards, and understand geological and climate systems, blending field surveys, lab work, and data modeling. The role leans on research methods, data analysis, attention to detail, and clear technical reporting. It suits investigative, realistic people drawn to the outdoors and to interpreting physical evidence. A bachelor's degree commonly opens the door (job zone 4). Growth is modest, about 5% through 2032, with wages roughly $70k-$120k, often higher in energy. People move in from environmental science or geology study and out toward environmental science, hydrology, data analysis, or resource and consulting roles.

title: Epidemiologist
text: An Epidemiologist studies how disease and injury spread through populations, designing studies, analyzing surveillance data, and turning results into public-health recommendations. The work is statistics-heavy, paired with data analysis, research methods, and the communication skills to brief agencies and policymakers. It fits investigative people with a social, public-service streak who like rigorous quantitative work with real-world impact. A master's or doctorate is typical (job zone 5). The outlook is strong, roughly 27% growth through 2032, with pay around $75k-$120k. People move in from biostatistics or public-health study and out toward biostatistics, data science, or health-policy analysis.

title: Biostatistician
text: A Biostatistician designs studies and applies statistical methods to medical, biological, and public-health data, building models and writing analysis code that drives clinical-trial conclusions and regulatory submissions. The core skills are statistics, data analysis, programming, and research methods, plus the writing needed to document results. It fits investigative, detail-oriented people who enjoy rigorous quantitative work in a scientific setting. An advanced degree is typical (job zone 5). Demand is very strong, around 30% growth through 2032, with pay roughly $85k-$140k. People often arrive from data analysis or epidemiology and move out toward data science, research science, or analytics leadership.

title: Electrician
text: An Electrician installs, maintains, and repairs electrical systems in residential, commercial, and industrial settings, reading blueprints, pulling wire, wiring panels, and troubleshooting faults to code. Core skills include electrical systems knowledge, mechanical skills, problem solving, and meticulous attention to detail, with customer-facing communication on service calls. It suits hands-on, practical people (Realistic) who like methodical, rule-following work (Conventional) and value steady demand over a desk. Outlook is growing steadily at roughly 6% through 2032, helped by construction and renewable-energy retrofits, with wages broadly in the $50k-$80k range. Entry is typically via apprenticeship rather than a four-year degree. Common moves are into solar installation, HVAC, maintenance supervision, or electrical engineering with further study.

title: Logistics Coordinator
text: A Logistics Coordinator plans and tracks the movement of goods, booking carriers, scheduling shipments, monitoring freight, and resolving delays so deliveries arrive on time and on budget. Core skills are logistics, operations, attention to detail, and clear communication across warehouses, drivers, suppliers, and customers, with light project management. It fits organized, detail-driven people (Conventional) who also enjoy coordinating and persuading others under pressure (Enterprising). The outlook is growing steadily at roughly 5% through 2032 as e-commerce and global supply chains expand, with wages broadly in the $45k-$70k range. Most enter from warehouse, dispatch, or customer-service roles. Common moves are into supply chain analysis, operations management, purchasing, or warehouse supervision.

title: HVAC Technician
text: An HVAC Technician installs, maintains, and repairs heating, cooling, ventilation, and refrigeration systems, diagnosing mechanical, refrigerant, and electrical faults and tuning equipment for efficiency. Core skills are mechanical skills, electrical systems, problem solving, attention to detail, and strong customer service on in-home calls. It suits hands-on, practical people (Realistic) with an investigative streak (Investigative) who enjoy diagnosing and fixing rather than routine assembly. Outlook is growing steadily at roughly 6% through 2032, supported by aging equipment, energy retrofits, and climate demand, with wages broadly in the $45k-$75k range. Entry is usually via apprenticeship or a trade certificate. Common moves are into electrical work, plumbing, building inspection, or maintenance supervision.

title: Plumber
text: A Plumber installs, maintains, and repairs water, gas, drainage, and heating pipe systems in homes and buildings, reading plans, joining pipe, locating leaks, and testing for pressure and safety to code. Core skills are mechanical skills, problem solving, attention to detail, and customer service, plus light project management on bids and jobs. It fits hands-on, practical people (Realistic) who like methodical, code-driven work (Conventional) and steady, recession-resistant demand. Outlook is growing steadily at roughly 6% through 2032 on construction and aging infrastructure, with wages broadly in the $50k-$80k range. Entry is typically via apprenticeship. Common moves are into pipefitting, HVAC, building inspection, or maintenance supervision, and many go on to own a licensed contracting business.

title: Welder
text: A Welder joins and cuts metal using arc, MIG, TIG, and gas processes for manufacturing, construction, and repair, reading drawings and weld symbols, setting up equipment, and inspecting joints for strength. Core skills are mechanical skills and exacting attention to detail, with problem solving and operations awareness on the shop floor. It suits steady-handed, practical people (Realistic) who like precise, standards-driven work (Conventional) and tangible finished output. Outlook is holding roughly steady, near 2% through 2032, though skilled and certified welders (pipe, underwater, aerospace) stay in demand, with wages broadly in the $40k-$65k range. Entry is via a trade program or on-the-job training. Common moves are into pipefitting, sheet metal, CNC machining, or welding inspection.

title: CNC Machinist
text: A CNC Machinist sets up and runs computer-controlled mills and lathes to produce precision metal and plastic parts from engineering drawings, loading programs, setting tooling and offsets, and measuring output to tight tolerances. Core skills are mechanical skills, exacting attention to detail, problem solving, systems thinking, and operations. It fits practical, precise people (Realistic) who like methodical, measurable work (Conventional) and reading drawings over customer contact. Outlook is holding roughly steady, near 2% through 2032, with automation reshaping but not eliminating the role and CAM-skilled machinists in demand; wages broadly fall in the $40k-$65k range. Entry is via a machining certificate or apprenticeship. Common moves are into tool and die making, welding, quality inspection, or manufacturing technician roles.

title: Heavy Truck Driver
text: A Heavy Truck Driver operates tractor-trailers to move freight over regional and long-haul routes, inspecting the vehicle, securing loads, logging hours, and delivering safely and on schedule. Core skills are operations and safety attention to detail, plus logistics awareness, customer service at docks, and communication with dispatch. It suits independent, practical people (Realistic) comfortable with routine and rules (Conventional) who prefer the road to an office. Outlook is growing steadily at roughly 4% through 2032, with persistent driver shortages keeping demand firm, and wages broadly in the $45k-$70k range, higher for specialized hauls. Entry requires a commercial driver's license. Common moves are into dispatch, logistics coordination, warehouse supervision, or becoming an owner-operator.

title: Warehouse Supervisor
text: A Warehouse Supervisor leads receiving, storage, picking, and shipping teams to move inventory accurately and on time, assigning shifts, tracking productivity and safety, and clearing bottlenecks across the floor. Core skills are operations, logistics, people management, communication, and attention to detail, with frontline leadership. It fits organized, take-charge people who enjoy directing others (Enterprising) within clear processes and metrics (Conventional). Outlook is growing steadily at roughly 5% through 2032 as e-commerce fulfillment expands, with wages broadly in the $45k-$70k range. Many rise from picker, forklift, or dispatch roles. Common moves are into logistics coordination, inventory management, supply chain analysis, or broader operations management.

title: Industrial Maintenance Mechanic
text: An Industrial Maintenance Mechanic keeps factory and warehouse machinery running, performing preventive maintenance and emergency repairs on conveyors, motors, hydraulics, and automated equipment to minimize production downtime. Core skills are mechanical skills, problem solving, electrical systems, attention to detail, and systems thinking across complex equipment. It suits hands-on, practical people (Realistic) with a diagnostic, investigative bent (Investigative) who like figuring out why a machine stopped. Outlook is growing steadily at roughly 5% through 2032 as automated facilities multiply, with wages broadly in the $50k-$75k range. Entry is via a trade program or apprenticeship. Common moves are into electrical work, HVAC, CNC machining, or maintenance supervision.

title: Lawyer
text: A Lawyer advises and represents individuals, businesses, or government agencies on legal matters, interpreting statutes and precedent to protect a client's interests. Core skills are legal reasoning, persuasive writing, legal research, negotiation, and public speaking, with relentless attention to detail. The role suits Enterprising and Investigative people who enjoy structured argument, advocacy, and digging through complex information, and who can handle high stakes and long hours. It requires a law degree and bar admission (O*NET job zone 5). The outlook is steady, roughly 8% growth through 2032, with an approximate US pay band of about $100k-$180k that varies widely by practice area and market. People often arrive from paralegal or policy roles and move on to compliance, judgeships, in-house counsel, or public-sector leadership.

title: Paralegal
text: A Paralegal performs substantive legal work under an attorney's supervision: drafting documents, organizing evidence, conducting research, and managing case files and filing deadlines. Key skills are meticulous attention to detail, legal research, clear writing, organization, and communication. The role fits Conventional and Investigative temperaments who like structure, accuracy, and supporting a larger effort without needing the spotlight. It typically needs an associate degree or certificate (O*NET job zone 3). Demand is roughly 4% growth through 2032, with an approximate US wage band near $50k-$75k. Many paralegals enter from administrative or office roles and advance toward compliance, contract administration, or law school and a Lawyer track.

title: Police Officer
text: A Police Officer protects communities by patrolling, responding to emergencies, enforcing laws, and investigating incidents, then documenting everything in reports that may be used in court. The work leans on communication, active listening, problem solving, situational attention, and conflict de-escalation. It suits Realistic and Social people who want hands-on, physical, public-facing work with a strong service motive and steadiness under pressure. Entry usually requires academy training and on-the-job preparation (O*NET job zone 3). The outlook is modest at roughly 3% growth through 2032, with an approximate US wage band of about $50k-$85k depending on jurisdiction and rank. Officers often advance to detective, supervisory, probation, or private security roles.

title: Probation Officer
text: A Probation Officer (community corrections specialist) supervises offenders in the community, enforcing court conditions while linking them to treatment, employment, and services to reduce reoffending. The job relies on active listening, clear report writing, risk assessment, problem solving, and steady communication. It suits Social and Enterprising people who can balance authority with genuine support and stay organized under caseload pressure. It typically requires a bachelor's degree plus training (O*NET job zone 4). The outlook is roughly flat at about 2% growth through 2032, with an approximate US wage band near $50k-$75k. Officers often come from social work or criminal-justice roles and move toward case management, counseling, or supervisory positions.

title: Compliance Officer
text: A Compliance Officer ensures an organization operates within laws, regulations, and ethical standards by building controls, running audits, investigating issues, and advising leadership on risk. Core skills are interpreting regulation, meticulous attention to detail, research, clear communication, and basic data analysis. The role fits Conventional and Enterprising people who like rules, systems, and accountability, and who can be diplomatic but firm. It generally needs a bachelor's degree and often domain certification (O*NET job zone 4). Outlook is roughly 5% growth through 2032, with an approximate US wage band of about $60k-$100k depending on industry. People arrive from legal, audit, or operations backgrounds and advance into risk leadership, legal, or policy roles.

title: Urban & Regional Planner
text: An Urban & Regional Planner shapes how communities grow, designing land-use, housing, and transportation plans that weigh economic, environmental, and social trade-offs. The role draws on data and geographic analysis, research, project management, writing, systems thinking, and public communication. It fits Investigative and Enterprising people who enjoy long-horizon problem solving and engaging diverse stakeholders in public meetings. It usually requires a master's degree (O*NET job zone 5). The outlook is roughly 4% growth through 2032, with an approximate US wage band near $65k-$100k. Planners often come from policy, geography, or engineering backgrounds and move into policy analysis, community management, or planning-director roles.

title: Policy Analyst
text: A Policy Analyst studies public problems and evaluates legislation, regulations, and programs, turning evidence into clear recommendations for decision-makers in government, think tanks, or advocacy groups. Core skills are rigorous research, data analysis, persuasive writing, systems thinking, and communication. The role suits Investigative and Enterprising people who like wrestling with complex social questions and influencing outcomes through argument rather than authority. It typically requires a master's degree in a relevant field (O*NET job zone 5). Outlook is roughly 6% growth through 2032, with an approximate US wage band near $60k-$95k. Analysts often come from law, planning, or research backgrounds and advance into program management, compliance, or leadership and elected-staff roles.
